title: Devin Ibanez becomes first openly gay Major League Rugby player
description: Rugby player Devin Ibanez came out as gay in 2020, the first player in the United States' Major League Rugby competition to do so openly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nyla Rose wins AEW Women's World Championship
description: Wrestler Nyla Rose took the AEW Women's World Championship on an episode of Dynamite in 2020, the first openly transgender woman to hold a world title in a major US wrestling promotion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pixar releases Out, its first short with a gay lead character
description: The animated short Out premiered in 2020 as the first Disney/Pixar production built around a gay main character and his coming-out story.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Owl House introduces Disney's first regular LGBT+ female animated leads
description: In 2020 the animated series The Owl House established Luz Noceda and Amity Blight as Disney's first canonically LGBT+ female regular characters in animation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hallmark airs its first movie centered on a same-sex couple
description: The Christmas House premiered in 2020 as the first Hallmark Channel film to give a same-sex couple a prominent storyline.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lifetime broadcasts its first LGBTQ-themed Christmas film
description: The Christmas Setup aired on Lifetime in 2020, the network's first holiday film with an LGBTQ storyline at its center.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jesse James Keitel plays first nonbinary series regular on US primetime TV
description: With the 2020 premiere of Big Sky, Jesse James Keitel became the first nonbinary actor cast as a nonbinary series regular on US primetime television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kipo character Benson delivers first explicit gay coming-out in all-ages animation
description: In 2020 the character Benson in Kipo and the Age of Wonderbeasts came out as gay on screen, the first explicit coming-out scene in an all-ages animated series.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Diana Zurco becomes Argentina's first openly transgender newscaster
description: Journalist Diana Zurco began anchoring news on Argentine television in 2020, the country's first openly transgender newscaster.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Camila Prins leads samba drum section at Sao Paulo Carnival
description: At the 2020 Carnival parade in Sao Paulo, Camila Prins became the first openly transgender woman to front the drum section of a top-tier samba school.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Valentina Sampaio is first openly trans model in SI Swimsuit Issue
description: Brazilian model Valentina Sampaio appeared in the 2020 Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue, the first openly transgender model featured in the publication.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rachel Slawson is first openly bisexual Miss USA contestant
description: Rachel Slawson competed for the Miss USA title in 2020 as the pageant's first openly bisexual contestant.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First LGBTQ marching band performs in Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
description: The Lesbian and Gay Big Apple Corps marched in the 2020 Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade in New York, the first LGBTQ marching band to appear in the event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Swiss voters approve same-sex marriage by referendum
description: Switzerland's electorate backed the Marriage for All law in a national referendum on 26 September 2021, clearing the way for same-sex marriage to take effect in July 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Six more Mexican states enact same-sex marriage
description: During 2021 same-sex marriage was passed and took effect in the Mexican states of Baja California, Sonora, Sinaloa, Queretaro, Guanajuato and Zacatecas, through codification, legislation or government decree.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile passes same-sex marriage law
description: Chile's congress approved a same-sex marriage law in 2021, with the legislation scheduled to take effect in March 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Yucatan passes same-sex marriage law
description: The Mexican state of Yucatan approved same-sex marriage legislation in 2021, with entry into force still pending at the time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil unions take effect in Montenegro
description: Montenegro's life-partnership law for same-sex couples came into effect in 2021, making it the first Western Balkan country to give same-sex couples legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Marino expands civil-union rights
description: In 2021 San Marino broadened the legal rights attached to civil unions for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bhutan decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Bhutan's revised penal code removing the criminalization of same-sex relations took effect in 2021 after receiving royal assent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anti-discrimination protections take effect in Angola
description: Angola's new penal code came into effect in 2021, adding protections against discrimination and hate crimes based on sexual orientation, including in employment.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Namibian court ruling extends anti-discrimination protection
description: A 2021 court ruling in Namibia extended anti-discrimination protection with regard to sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Botswana appeals court upholds decriminalization ruling
description: In 2021 Botswana's highest appeals court upheld the 2019 High Court judgment that struck down the criminalization of same-sex relations, making the decriminalization final.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kamala Harris marches in Capital Pride as sitting US Vice President
description: In 2021 Kamala Harris joined the Capital Pride Walk in Washington, D.C., the first sitting US Vice President to take part in an LGBTQ+ Pride march.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eduardo Leite becomes Brazil's first openly gay governor
description: Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite came out publicly in July 2021, the first sitting governor in Brazilian history to be openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Greece appoints its first openly gay minister
description: Nicholas Yatromanolakis took ministerial rank in the Greek government in 2021, the first openly gay person to do so in Greece.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pete Buttigieg joins US Cabinet as first openly gay Senate-confirmed member
description: Pete Buttigieg was confirmed as US Secretary of Transportation in 2021, the first openly gay person confirmed by the Senate to a Cabinet post and the first openly gay non-acting Cabinet member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Aung Myo Min becomes Myanmar's first openly gay cabinet minister
description: Human-rights advocate Aung Myo Min joined Myanmar's National Unity Government cabinet in 2021, the country's first openly gay minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alexandra Briem elected President of Reykjavik City Council
description: Alexandra Briem was elected President of the Reykjavik City Council in 2021, becoming Iceland's first openly transgender political officeholder.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Christy Abizaid leads US National Counterterrorism Center
description: Christy Abizaid took office as director of the US National Counterterrorism Center in 2021, the first openly gay person to head the agency.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gottmik competes on RuPaul's Drag Race as first openly trans man
description: Drag artist Gottmik joined the 2021 season of RuPaul's Drag Race, the first openly transgender man to compete on the show.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tashnuva Anan Shishir becomes Bangladesh's first openly trans news anchor
description: Tashnuva Anan Shishir began presenting television news in Bangladesh in 2021, the country's first openly transgender news anchor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Elliot Page appears on cover of Time magazine
description: Actor Elliot Page was featured on the cover of Time in 2021, the first openly trans man on the magazine's cover.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Country musician TJ Osborne comes out as gay
description: TJ Osborne of the duo Brothers Osborne came out publicly on 3 February 2021, the first openly gay artist signed to a major country music label.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patti Harrison voices role in Raya and the Last Dragon
description: Comedian Patti Harrison voiced the minor role of Tail Chief in Disney's Raya and the Last Dragon in 2021, the first known transgender actor to appear in a Disney animated film.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rachel Levine confirmed as US assistant secretary for health
description: The US Senate confirmed Rachel Levine as assistant secretary for health on 24 March 2021, the first openly transgender person confirmed by the Senate to a federal post.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Martine Delaney inducted into Tasmanian Honour Roll of Women
description: Activist Martine Delaney joined the Tasmanian Honour Roll of Women in 2021, the first openly transgender woman to receive the honour.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US President proclaims Transgender Day of Visibility
description: In 2021 Joe Biden issued the first formal US presidential proclamation recognizing the Transgender Day of Visibility.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Adrian Hanstock heads British Transport Police
description: Adrian Hanstock was appointed temporary Chief Constable of the British Transport Police in 2021, the first openly gay man to lead a British police force.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alana Gisele Banks elected to US public school board
description: Alana Gisele Banks won election to a public school board in 2021, the first Black openly transgender woman elected to such a board in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First gay couple on the cover of The Knot
description: Jonathan Bennett and Jaymes Vaughan appeared on the cover of the wedding magazine The Knot in 2021, the first gay couple featured there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Megan Rohrer installed as bishop in Evangelical Lutheran Church in America
description: Megan Rohrer was installed as a bishop of the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America in 2021, the first openly transgender and nonbinary bishop of any Christian denomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dancing with the Stars features first same-sex dance pairing in US version
description: The US edition of Dancing with the Stars presented its first same-sex dance partnership in 2021.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pennsylvania governor recognizes Bisexual Pride Day
description: Governor Tom Wolf of Pennsylvania issued a statement recognizing Bisexual Pride Day in 2021, the first US governor to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: DC Comics introduces first openly transgender Amazon in Wonder Woman
description: The character Bia debuted in DC Comics' Wonder Woman series in 2021 as the first openly transgender Amazon.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ariel Nicholson is first openly transgender person on a Vogue cover
description: Model Ariel Nicholson appeared on the cover of Vogue in 2021, the first openly transgender person featured there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colton Underwood comes out as gay
description: Former Bachelor lead Colton Underwood came out publicly on 14 April 2021, the first openly gay lead in the franchise's history and the second openly gay NFL free agent after Michael Sam.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Carl Nassib becomes first active NFL player to come out as gay
description: NFL defensive lineman Carl Nassib came out publicly on 21 June 2021, the first active player in the league's history to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luke Prokop comes out as gay while under NHL contract
description: Canadian prospect Luke Prokop, signed by the Nashville Predators after the 2020 NHL entry draft, came out in 2021 as the first active player under a National Hockey League contract to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Quinn wins gold as first openly trans, nonbinary Olympian
description: Canadian footballer Quinn competed at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021 as the first openly transgender, nonbinary athlete at the Games, and took gold with the Canadian women's team — the first such athlete to medal.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Slovenia
description: Same-sex marriage became legal in Slovenia in July 2022 following a constitutional-court decision, with parliament codifying the change in October 2022 — the first country in Eastern Europe to allow it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cuba approves same-sex marriage by referendum
description: Cuban voters approved a new Family Code in a 2022 referendum, legalizing same-sex marriage in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage becomes legal nationwide in Mexico
description: With Durango, Guerrero, the State of Mexico, Tabasco, Tamaulipas, Veracruz and Yucatan completing legalization in 2022, same-sex marriage became available in every Mexican state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
